message: So there I was, 10 minutes into a surprise thunderstorm, when my family group chat absolutely *exploded*. My sister: 'WHERE'S THE FLASHLIGHT?' Mom: 'Did anyone check the basement window?' Dad: three fire emojis and nothing else. My nephew: 'can we call this Hurricane Kevin.' It was chaos—but honestly? That frantic texting meant everyone was *thinking* about what mattered. Once the storm passed, I realized we'd kind of stumbled into a half-baked prep check without even planning it. No fancy emergency plan needed—just the fact that we were all asking the right questions in real time made a difference. The best readiness doesn't have to be stressful or complicated. Sometimes it's just knowing where your family keeps stuff and being willing to ask. Do you have a go-to person in your family who's the unofficial 'storm coordinator'? 😊
